quiz.title: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus Quiz
quiz.subtitle: Advanced FTC with Chain Rule and Complex Functions (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Calculus
quiz.topic: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus - Harder Level

questions.question: Evaluate \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{x}^{x^3} \frac{\sin t}{t} \, dt\). Simplify your answer.
questions.explanation: By FTC with variable limits: \(\frac{d}{dx}[F(x^3)-F(x)] = f(x^3) \cdot 3x^2 - f(x) \cdot 1\). Here \(f(t) = \frac{\sin t}{t}\), so the derivative is \(\frac{\sin(x^3)}{x^3} \cdot 3x^2 - \frac{\sin x}{x} = \frac{3\sin(x^3)}{x} - \frac{\sin x}{x} = \frac{3\sin(x^3) - \sin x}{x}\).

questions.question: Determine \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{\cos x}^{\sin x} e^{t^2} \, dt\).
questions.explanation: By FTC: \(\frac{d}{dx}[F(\sin x)-F(\cos x)] = f(\sin x) \cdot \cos x - f(\cos x) \cdot (-\sin x) = e^{(\sin x)^2} \cos x + e^{(\cos x)^2} \sin x = e^{\sin^2 x} \cos x + e^{\cos^2 x} \sin x\).

questions.question: Evaluate \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{x^2}^{x^4} \frac{1}{\sqrt{1 + t^2}} \, dt\). Simplify your answer.
Simplify \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{x^2}^{\sqrt{x}} \frac{1}{\ln t} \, dt\).
questions.explanation: By FTC: \(\frac{d}{dx}[F(\sqrt{x})-F(x^2)] = f(\sqrt{x}) \cdot \frac{1}{2\sqrt{x}} - f(x^2) \cdot 2x = \frac{1}{\ln(\sqrt{x})} \cdot \frac{1}{2\sqrt{x}} - \frac{1}{\ln(x^2)} \cdot 2x\). Using \(\ln(\sqrt{x}) = \frac{1}{2}\ln x\) and \(\ln(x^2) = 2\ln x\), this becomes \(\frac{2}{\ln x} \cdot \frac{1}{2\sqrt{x}} - \frac{1}{2\ln x} \cdot 2x = \frac{1}{\sqrt{x}\ln x} - \frac{x}{\ln x} = \frac{1 - x^{3/2}}{\sqrt{x}\ln x}\).

questions.question: Find \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{2x}^{x^2} \ln(1 + t^2) \, dt\). Simplify your answer.
Evaluate \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{0}^{x} \left( \int_{0}^{t} e^{u^2} \, du \right) dt\).
questions.explanation: Let \(F(t) = \int_{0}^{t} e^{u^2} du\). Then the given integral is \(\int_{0}^{x} F(t) dt\). By FTC Part 1, \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{0}^{x} F(t) dt = F(x) = \int_{0}^{x} e^{u^2} du\).

questions.question: Determine \(\frac{d}{dx} \int_{x^2}^{x^3} \frac{1}{\ln t} \, dt\) for \(x > 0, x \neq 1\).
questions.explanation: By FTC: \(\frac{d}{dx}[F(x^3)-F(x^2)] = f(x^3) \cdot 3x^2 - f(x^2) \cdot 2x = \frac{1}{\ln(x^3)} \cdot 3x^2 - \frac{1}{\ln(x^2)} \cdot 2x = \frac{1}{3\ln x} \cdot 3x^2 - \frac{1}{2\ln x} \cdot 2x = \frac{x^2}{\ln x} - \frac{x}{\ln x} = \frac{x^2 - x}{\ln x} = \frac{x(x-1)}{\ln x}\).

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered advanced FTC applications with complex functions.
scoreMessages.70: Excellent work! You understand challenging FTC problems well. Review the ones you missed.
scoreMessages.50: Good effort! Keep practicing with variable limits and nested integrals.
scoreMessages.0: Review FTC with both limits as functions and practice simplifying complex expressions.
